replies.msg:
https://www.legco.gov.hk/general/english/bills/bill_1620.htm

Sixth Legislative Council (2016 - 2020)
The Government is primarily responsible for proposing new legislation or amendments to existing legislation, in the form of bills which are considered by the Council for enactment. Subject to meeting certain conditions, Members of the Council may also introduce bills into the Council.

Passage of a bill

A bill is first published in the Gazette before it is introduced into the Council. It has to be given three readings for its passage by the Council. The First Reading is a formality, with the Clerk reading the short title of a bill at a Council meeting. The Second Reading of the bill starts with the government official or Member who introduces the bill moving the motion "That the bill be read the second time" and explaining the purpose of the bill. Usually, after the motion has been moved, the debate on it is adjourned and the bill is referred to the House Committee to allow Members more time to study it, either in the House Committee or in a bills committee set up by the House Committee specifically for it.

The debate on the Second Reading resumes at a subsequent Council meeting after the bill has been studied by the House Committee or a bills committee. During the debate, Members present their views on the general merits and principles of the bill and may indicate their support or otherwise. A vote is then taken by the Council on the motion "That the bill be read the second time". If the motion is not passed, the bill cannot proceed further. If it is passed, the bill is given the Second Reading, and the Council will then sit as a "Committee of the whole Council" during which the Committee goes through the clauses of the bill, making amendments where agreed to by the Committee. After the bill has passed through the Committee of the whole Council with or without amendments, it is reported back to the Council for it to consider whether to support the passage of the bill by giving it the Third Reading. Usually, the resumption of the Second Reading debate and the Third Reading (if the bill has been given the Second Reading) take place at the same Council meeting.

When a bill has been given three readings, it becomes an Ordinance enacted by the Legislative Council. Unless a later date is specified, it may take effect after it has been signed by the Chief Executive and published in the Gazette.

For those who are still taking CONSERVATIVE ways, here is your endgame, 12 Jun 2019.
獨裁 屌你老母

replies.msg: 一百萬人又點，9成9和理非鳩行完當做咗野，當中有幾千人衝擊政府圍欄黑警就話唔守秩序影衰香港人。依家政府已經睇穿港豬

1. 要係12號前就出現，否則當日早上立法會過左就做乜都冇用。

2. 立法會係首要地點。

3. 請不要單獨行動，最少5人1組，10人以上更好。
・如果無朋友，即時join現場既其他人，大家都唔會拒絕。
・記得自己組員既名，就算比人捉左都可以用佢全名幫佢。
・組員被警圍時，其他組員立即幫拖推開。
・齊心整跌警察盾牌及警棍防止被打
・組與組之間互相幫助
・盡量避免帶單邊黑色耳機既人

4. 長期佔領，物資糧水係必不可缺。
・設立補給點
・足夠物資供應

5. 保護裝備（眼，口罩，保鮮紙）

4，5號point我唔熟
希望大家可以補充

大家要出黎
就一定要做好準備先出黎
如果唔係好似尋日咁
乜都冇
冇目標，得對手，人群分散
只會比狗輕易趕走

・要係警力未多時到立法會先發制人
- 必須於12號前出現
・足夠前線同警對抗
- 必定會受傷
・足夠人流阻塞附近唔好比警方有機開路
- 比現場警力多4-5倍，塞住車同警
・一班人負責立法會行動
- 指揮路向，確立目標

以上全為發夢所得，尋日睇完新聞就發左成晚夢。

冇咩奇怪，政府就算見你咁多人出嚟都唔會即刻話撤，因為咁所有功勞就比泛民主攞晒，點都要扮多一兩日，比時間獻世派“勸退”，等獻世派攞返啲尾彩。
當然刁帝都可能會繼續照去馬，各國反應同國際資金撤離香港好難估計會唔會，但出年個垃圾會獻世派應該會大敗，到時可能剩返功能組別嘅否決權。泛民主導到議會政府就頭痕，第一時間對所有高官不信任動議應該出晒嚟，當然最後因為功能組別過唔到。不過政府嘅嘢包括財政預算之類都應該唔駛點過，除咗一啲民生議案，尤其是明日大愚補血劍一定斷。

當然刁帝都可能會繼續照去馬，各國反應同國際資金撤離香港好難估計會唔會，但出年個垃圾會獻世派應該會大敗，到時可能剩返功能組別嘅否決權。泛民主導到議會政府就頭痕，第一時間對所有高官不信任動議應該出晒嚟，當然最後因為功能組別過唔到。不過政府嘅嘢包括財政預算之類都應該唔駛點過，除咗一啲民生議案，尤其是明日大愚補血劍一定斷。
